description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a metal framework. The solar panels are arranged in a single, continuous row, elevated above a patterned base that may represent a paved or reinforced surface. The supporting structure consists of angled metal posts and cross-bracing, providing stability and ensuring the panels are optimally tilted for sunlight exposure. The base area beneath the panels is open, allowing for airflow and possibly maintenance access. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, and the structure appears fixed and robust, suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, parks, or public spaces. The overall design emphasizes durability and efficient solar energy capture.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a sturdy metal frame and a patterned base, designed for efficient solar energy collection in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting posts, with the patterned base visible beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled support structure and the length of the solar panel array, with the patterned base extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the supporting framework, with the base pattern visible below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled supports and the full length of the array, with the patterned base extending to the side.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts and cross-bracing
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8-10 panels
